questions.text: The horizontal axis on a coordinate plane is called the:
questions.explanation: The horizontal axis is called the x-axis. The vertical axis is the y-axis.

questions.text: The point where the x-axis and y-axis meet is called the:
questions.explanation: The point of intersection of the x-axis and y-axis is called the origin, denoted as O(0, 0).

questions.text: The coordinates of the origin are:
questions.explanation: The origin is the point where both axes intersect. Its coordinates are (0, 0).

questions.text: In the ordered pair (3, 5), the value 3 is called the:
questions.explanation: In an ordered pair (x, y), the first value is the abscissa (x-coordinate) and the second is the ordinate (y-coordinate).

questions.text: The point (0, 5) lies on which axis?
questions.explanation: Any point with x-coordinate = 0 lies on the y-axis. (0, 5) has x = 0, so it lies on the y-axis.

questions.text: The point (4, 0) lies on which axis?
questions.explanation: Any point with y-coordinate = 0 lies on the x-axis. (4, 0) has y = 0, so it lies on the x-axis.

questions.text: Which of the following points lies in the second quadrant?
questions.explanation: Second quadrant: x is negative, y is positive. (-3, 4) has x < 0 and y > 0 → second quadrant.

questions.text: A linear graph shows the relationship between two quantities. If it is a straight line passing through the origin, it means:

options.b: The quantities are in direct proportion
options.c: The quantities are in inverse proportion

questions.explanation: A straight line through the origin means y = kx (direct proportion). As x increases, y increases proportionally.

questions.text: On a graph, the distance between two points (2, 0) and (8, 0) is:
questions.explanation: Both points lie on the x-axis. Distance = 8 − 2 = 6 units.

questions.text: In the third quadrant, the signs of coordinates are:
questions.explanation: Third quadrant: both x and y are negative. Signs: (−, −).

questions.text: A car travels at a constant speed. The graph of distance vs time will be:

options.b: A straight line through the origin

questions.explanation: At constant speed, distance = speed × time. This is direct proportion (y = kx), giving a straight line through the origin.

questions.text: The point (-4, -7) is in which quadrant?
questions.explanation: (-4, -7): x is negative, y is negative → third quadrant.

questions.text: Which point does NOT satisfy the equation y = 2x?
questions.explanation: Check (3, 5): y = 2 × 3 = 6 ≠ 5. So (3, 5) does not lie on y = 2x.

questions.text: A graph showing the temperature of a city over 7 days is best displayed as a:
questions.explanation: A line graph is best for showing how a quantity changes over time (like temperature over days).

questions.text: If y = 3x, when x = 4, y = ?
The number of quadrants in the coordinate plane is:
questions.explanation: The x-axis and y-axis divide the plane into 4 quadrants: I, II, III, IV.

questions.text: The x-coordinate of any point on the y-axis is always:
questions.explanation: All points on the y-axis have x-coordinate = 0.

questions.text: A graph showing profit of a company from 2018 to 2024 uses the x-axis for years and y-axis for profit. The years are plotted on the:
questions.explanation: The independent variable (years) is conventionally plotted on the x-axis (horizontal axis).

questions.text: Which of the following lies in the fourth quadrant?
questions.explanation: Fourth quadrant: x is positive, y is negative. (5, -3) has x > 0 and y < 0 → fourth quadrant.

questions.text: If the graph of x versus y is a straight line NOT passing through the origin, the relationship is:
questions.explanation: A straight line not through the origin means y = mx + c (c ≠ 0). This is a linear relationship but NOT direct proportion (which requires the line to pass through origin).

questions.text: A car travels at 60 km/h. Using the linear graph of distance vs time, the distance covered in 2.5 hours is:
questions.explanation: Distance = speed × time = 60 × 2.5 = 150 km.

questions.text: In the figure, a line graph shows Riya's savings: Jan=500, Feb=800, Mar=1100, Apr=1400. The pattern suggests savings increase by ₹___ each month. In May, savings will be:
questions.explanation: Each month savings increase by 300. May = 1400 + 300 = ₹1700.

questions.text: Three vertices of a rectangle are at (1, 2), (5, 2) and (5, 6). The fourth vertex is at:
questions.explanation: The given points form three corners. The rectangle has sides parallel to axes. The fourth vertex must be at (1, 6) to complete the rectangle.

questions.text: The graph of y = x + 3 passes through which of the following points?
The following table gives time vs distance for a cyclist: Time(h): 1, 2, 3, 4. Distance(km): 15, 30, 45, 60. What type of graph does this produce?
questions.explanation: Distance = 15 × time. This is direct proportion (y = 15x), giving a straight line through the origin.

questions.text: Points A(2, 3), B(2, 7) are plotted. The distance AB is:
questions.explanation: Both points have x = 2 (vertical line). Distance = difference in y = 7 − 3 = 4 units.

questions.text: A line graph shows a patient's temperature: 8am=38°C, 10am=39°C, 12pm=40°C, 2pm=39°C, 4pm=37°C. Between which times did temperature fall the most?
questions.explanation: Temperature changes: 8-10: +1, 10-12: +1, 12-2: −1, 2-4: −2. Greatest fall = 2pm to 4pm (−2°C).

questions.text: If y = 5x and the graph passes through (a, 20), find a.
In a graph, the x-axis represents number of items sold and y-axis represents total cost. If the line passes through (0, 50) and (5, 200), the cost per item is:
questions.explanation: From (0,50) to (5,200): change in cost = 200−50 = 150 for 5 items. Cost per item = 150/5 = ₹30.

questions.text: In the figure, four points are plotted: P(3, 4), Q(-3, 4), R(-3, -4), S(3, -4). What shape do these four points form?
questions.explanation: Width = 6 (3 to -3 in x). Height = 8 (4 to -4 in y). Sides parallel to axes → rectangle.

questions.text: A line graph shows population (in lakhs): 2018=12, 2019=14, 2020=16, 2021=18, 2022=20. If the trend continues, in which year will population first exceed 25 lakhs?
questions.explanation: Population increases by 2 lakhs/year. 2022=20, 2023=22, 2024=24, 2025=26 > 25. First year exceeding 25 lakhs = 2025.

questions.text: In the figure, a line graph shows a car's journey: from 0-1h it covers 60 km, then stops for 30 min, then covers 60 km in 1h. The average speed for the entire journey (150 min total) is:
questions.explanation: Total distance = 120 km. Total time = 150 min = 2.5 h. Average speed = 120/2.5 = 48 km/h.

questions.text: A graph of cost vs number of items shows: 10 items cost ₹450, 20 items cost ₹900, 30 items cost ₹1350. This line, when extended, passes through the origin. The cost of 45 items is:
questions.explanation: Cost per item = 450/10 = ₹45. Cost of 45 items = 45 × 45 = ₹2025.

questions.text: In the figure, a distance-time graph shows: segment A (horizontal) means the object is at rest, segment B (steep rise) means the object is moving fast, segment C (gentle rise) means the object is moving slowly. Which order shows the correct interpretation of a graph showing: rest → slow → fast?
questions.explanation: Rest = horizontal (no distance change). Slow = gentle slope (small distance per unit time). Fast = steep slope (large distance per unit time). Order: horizontal → gentle → steep.

questions.text: A line graph shows a company's sales (₹ in thousands): Jan=40, Feb=50, Mar=45, Apr=60, May=55, Jun=70. The months showing an increase over the previous month are:
questions.explanation: Feb(50>40)↑, Mar(45<50)↓, Apr(60>45)↑, May(55<60)↓, Jun(70>55)↑. Increases: Feb, Apr, Jun.

questions.text: In the figure, two lines are drawn on the same graph. Line 1: y = 2x. Line 2: y = x + 4. These two lines intersect at the point:
questions.explanation: At intersection: 2x = x + 4 → x = 4. y = 2 × 4 = 8. Point = (4, 8). Verify on line 2: y = 4+4 = 8 ✓.

questions.text: Using graph of y = 4x, find y when x = 7.
A line passes through (0, 0) and (3, 12). What is the y-value when x = 5?
questions.explanation: Slope = 12/3 = 4. Equation: y = 4x. When x = 5: y = 20.

questions.text: Plot the points (2, 3) and (6, 3) on a graph. What is the distance between them?
questions.explanation: Both have y = 3 (horizontal line). Distance = 6 − 2 = 4 units.

questions.text: A taxi charges ₹20 fixed + ₹15 per km. Write the equation and find the cost for 8 km.
The graph of a linear equation passes through (1, 5) and (3, 11). Find the value of y when x = 0.
The vertices of a triangle are at (1, 1), (4, 1) and (4, 5). What is the area of the triangle? (in sq units)
questions.explanation: Base = 4−1 = 3 units (horizontal, from (1,1) to (4,1)). Height = 5−1 = 4 units (vertical, from (4,1) to (4,5)). Area = ½ × 3 × 4 = 6 sq units.

questions.text: In a line graph showing temperature over 5 days: Mon=22°C, Tue=25°C, Wed=20°C, Thu=28°C, Fri=24°C. What is the mean temperature over these 5 days?
A graph of principal vs simple interest (at 10% for 1 year) passes through origin. If principal = ₹3500, what is the SI? (in ₹)
Two lines y = 3x and y = x + 8 intersect. Find the x-coordinate of the intersection point.
A rectangle has vertices at A(0,0), B(5,0), C(5,3) and D(0,3). Find the area of the rectangle. (in sq units)
questions.explanation: Length = 5 units (along x-axis). Breadth = 3 units (along y-axis). Area = 5 × 3 = 15 sq units.

cases.case_text: Arjun cycles to school every day. The distance-time graph of his journey shows: at t=0 min, d=0 km; at t=10 min, d=2 km; at t=20 min, d=4 km; at t=30 min, d=4 km (stationary); at t=40 min, d=6 km.

subparts.text: During which time period was Arjun stationary?
subparts.explanation: From t=20 to t=30, distance stayed at 4 km (no change). Arjun was stationary during 20 to 30 min.

subparts.text: What was Arjun's speed (in km/min) during the first 20 minutes?
subparts.explanation: Distance in first 20 min = 4 km. Speed = 4/20 = 0.2 km/min.

subparts.text: Total distance covered in 40 minutes is 6 km. What is average speed in km/h?

cases.case_text: A shopkeeper records his daily sales for a week on a line graph. Sales (in ₹): Mon=1200, Tue=1500, Wed=1800, Thu=1400, Fri=2000, Sat=2400, Sun=2000. He wants to analyse trends.

subparts.text: On which day did sales decrease compared to the previous day?
subparts.explanation: Thu=1400 < Wed=1800 → decrease on Thursday. Also Sun=2000 < Sat=2400 (decrease on Sunday).

subparts.text: What was the total sales for the week? (in ₹)
The mean daily sales for the week is: (in ₹)
